title: xyris subsetigera
intro: Xyris subsetigera, also known as the Cape grass or yellow-eyed grass, is a species of flowering plant native to South Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Xyris subsetigera include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and ointments.
